justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified as blocker in logging table and recommendations #1, (2) API call duration gap identified in logging table, (3) Metric label (valid/invalid) gap identified in metrics table and recommendation #3, (4) Latency histogram absence noted as blocker in metrics table and recommendation #2, (5) PII consideration flagged appropriately in recommendation #6 with 'redact if PII policy requires it' rather than blindly recommending logging raw addresses, (6) Verdict is NOT READY with clear blockers, (7) Recommendations are highly specific and actionable with concrete examples.

observations.text: Request ID gap identified prominently as a blocker with vivid operational scenario ('At 3am with 500 concurrent requests')
API call duration gap clearly identified in logging table and tied to latency degradation detection
Metric label gap identified precisely — 'Counter exists but has no result label — cannot distinguish valid vs. invalid vs. error rates'
Latency histogram absence called out as a blocker with SLO and p99 implications
PII consideration handled correctly — recommendation #6 says 'redact if PII policy requires it' rather than unconditionally recommending logging raw addresses
Verdict is NOT READY with three clearly stated blockers

Recommendations are exceptionally specific: 'Add a request ID (UUID) to every log line', 'Add a result label to validation_requests_total (valid, invalid, error, timeout)', exact metric names suggested
Goes well beyond minimum requirements — resilience assessment, circuit breaker analysis, health check flapping risk, and escalation routing to other agents
Prioritization is clear with must-have/should-have/nice-to-have tiers

# Pippen's Stability & Operational Readiness Review
## Address Validation Microservice

---

### Integration Assessment

**Component Interactions:**

| From | To | Status | Issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| Client | POST /validate | **Risk** | No request ID propagated — impossible to correlate client-side errors with server-side logs |
| /validate handler | SmartyStreets API | **Risk** | 3s timeout exists (good), but no retry policy and no circuit breaker — a SmartyStreets outage will burn through res
